skill.name: write-a-skill
skill.description: Create new agent skills with proper structure, trigger descriptions, progressive disclosure, examples, and optional bundled resources.
skill.summary: Guides users through designing, drafting, and reviewing structured agent skills.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown fences and description text as shell execution and weak cryptography. Review found these are false positives in documentation examples, with no executable code, network access, secret access, or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Examples Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged fenced Markdown examples and tree diagrams as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines are documentation examples only and do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced locations are Markdown fence delimiters and example text. No command invocation, interpreter call, or user-controlled shell path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Description Wording Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged description-related wording as weak cryptography. The reviewed lines contain skill description guidance and a checklist item, not cryptographic algorithms or hashing code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines discuss descriptions, triggers, and review criteria. I found no cryptographic API, weak hash name, encoded payload, or security-sensitive transformation.

content.value_statement: Creating reusable agent skills requires clear triggers, concise instructions, and scoped supporting files. This skill guides users through drafting, organizing, and reviewing a complete skill package.
actual_capabilities: Guides requirement gathering for a new agent skill.
Defines a simple SKILL.md structure with optional reference files.
Explains how to write trigger-focused skill descriptions.
Suggests when deterministic helper scripts are useful.
Provides a review checklist for draft quality.
limitations: It does not generate a complete marketplace manifest by itself.
It does not validate package security or dependency safety.
It provides authoring guidance, not a runtime skill loader.
It expects the user to review domain-specific accuracy.

use_cases.title: Draft a New Team Skill
use_cases.description: Create a focused skill for repeated internal workflows with clear triggers and concise instructions.

use_cases.title: Package Domain Knowledge
use_cases.description: Turn long process notes into a skill with optional references and examples.

use_cases.title: Improve Skill Quality
use_cases.description: Review an existing skill draft for structure, terminology, examples, and disclosure depth.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple reusable skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a new skill for [task]. Ask me the key questions first, then draft the SKILL.md structure.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Notes Into a Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request using existing process material.
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn these workflow notes into an agent skill. Keep the main instructions concise and suggest reference files only when needed.

prompt_templates.title: Design Skill Resources
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for multi-file skill organization.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a skill package for [domain]. Decide what belongs in SKILL.md, examples, references, and optional helper scripts.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Skill Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill draft for trigger clarity, progressive disclosure, missing examples, and unnecessary complexity.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for writing release notes from merged pull requests.
output_examples.output: A concise skill plan with trigger wording, core workflow steps, optional examples, and a review checklist.

output_examples.input: Review my customer support escalation skill draft.
output_examples.output: A structured review covering description clarity, file split decisions, example quality, and missing edge cases.

output_examples.input: Turn this onboarding process into a reusable skill.
output_examples.output: A recommended skill layout with main instructions, reference material, and guidance for deterministic helper scripts.

best_practices: Write the description so the agent knows exactly when to load the skill.
Keep SKILL.md concise and move rare details into linked reference files.
Add scripts only for deterministic tasks that improve reliability.
anti_patterns: Do not use vague descriptions that could match many unrelated tasks.
Do not place every detail in SKILL.md when references would be clearer.
Do not add helper scripts without a specific repeated operation.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create agent skills with clear descriptions, focused instructions, examples, references, and optional scripts.

faq.question: Does it write complete skills automatically?
faq.answer: It guides drafting and review. The user still provides the task context and approves the final content.

faq.question: When should I split content into reference files?
faq.answer: Split content when SKILL.md becomes long, has distinct domains, or contains details used only in advanced cases.

faq.question: When should a skill include scripts?
faq.answer: Include scripts for deterministic validation, formatting, or repeated operations that benefit from explicit error handling.

faq.question: Can this skill help with Claude and Codex workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. It gives general skill authoring guidance that can support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code skill workflows.

faq.question: Does this skill perform security validation?
faq.answer: No. It includes a drafting checklist, but separate security review is required before publishing or installing a skill.
